title: The Fragile Mind
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A woman discovers she has eight alternate identities and must learn to know the parts of herself she never knew existed.
long_description:
Mara Elford is thirty-four and has been losing time for years. She wakes in parking lots, finds meals she didn't cook, reads journal entries in handwriting that isn't hers. When her sister insists she seek treatment, Mara enters the care of Dr. James Whitfield, a psychiatrist who specializes in dissociative identity disorder — and begins the extraordinary process of meeting the eight alternate identities she has been sharing her life with, unknown, since childhood.

There is Nell, the seven-year-old who lives in the memory of a summer garden and makes pasta when she is lonely. There is Marcus, the furious teenager who has kept a terrible secret for twenty years. There is Sylvie, the quiet librarian who has been managing the system's information for decades. And there is Vera, who cannot speak but draws her way toward a door she has been approaching for years.

The Fragile Mind is a novel about the mind's extraordinary capacity for self-protection, about the community that forms inside a shattered self, and about the long work of coming home to a life that has always, in its way, been yours. Told through therapy sessions, journal entries, and the slow accumulation of trust, it asks what it means to love a self you are only beginning to know.
